properties.remark: QUARTER TO HALF DOLLAR SIZE HAIL REPORTED IN GRAVEL HILL.
TREES DOWN AT WHITE OAK LAKE ACROSS HIGHWAY 57
SEVERAL TREES WERE BLOWN DOWN NEAR HIGHWAY 31.

THE EF2 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN 3 WSW OF THORNTON AND CONTINUED 19.2 MILES TO 4.9 SW OF RISON...AFFECTING CALHOUN...DALLAS AND CLEVELAND COUNTIES. IN CALHOUN COUNTY...DAMAGE WAS MOSTLY LIMITED TO TIMBER. IN DALLAS COUNTY...THERE WAS SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE TO HOUSES NORTHWEST OF FORDYCE...THE COUNTRY CLUB...AND A SET OF TRANSMISSION TOWERS. IN CLEVELAND COUNTY...THERE WAS MOSTLY DAMAGE TO TIMBER. HOWEVER...A CHURCH BUILT IN 1852 IN NORTH KINGSLAND WAS DESTROYED.
HAIL CRACKED THE WINDSHIELD OF THE AMATUER RADIO OPERATOR.
TORNADO REPORTED ON THE GROUND BY LAW ENFORCEMENT4 MILES NORTHEAST OF FORDYCE.
2 HOUSES DAMAGAED ON HIGHWAY 8 NORTHWEST OF FORDYCE. NO INJURIES REPORTED AT THIS TIME.

THIS TORNADO STARTED 2.5 MILES SW OF COY AND ENDED 1.7 MILES SW OF COY FOR A TOTAL PATH LENGTH OF THREE QUARTERS OF A MILE AND WAS RATED AN EF1. DAMAGE FOUND INCLUDED AN IRRIGATION PIVOT THAT WAS FLIPPED OVER AND LANDED IN A NEARBY DITCH. A NUMBER OF TREE LIMBS WERE SNAPPED OFF.
AMATEUER OPERATOR REPORTING SOME HAIL STONES THE SIZE OF SAOFTBALLS.
AMATEUR RADIO OPERATOR REPORTED A BRIEF TORNADO TOUCHDOWWN 5 MILES WEST OF COY
AMATEUR RADIO OPERATOR REPORTS A TORNADO ON THE GROUND 2 MILES NORTH OF SLOVAK.
TREES WERE BLOWN DOWN.
GOLF BALL TO BASEBALL SIZE HAIL REPORTED IN NEWPORT
GOLF BALL TO BASEBALL SIZE HAIL REPORTED IN NEWPORT

THE STARTING POINT OF THE TORNADO WAS 2.7 MILES WSW OF MOSCOW AND ENDED 1.2 MILES NE OF MOSCOW FOR A TOTAL PATH LENGTH OF 3.8 MILES. THIS TORNADO WAS RATED AN EF1. DAMAGE FOUND INCLUDED A SHED WHICH WAS THROWN OVER A FARM SHOP. THE FARM SHOPS ROOF WAS DAMAGED. AN EMPTY 12000 GALLON FUEL TANK WAS THROWN INTO A FIELD. AN IRRIGATION PIVOT WAS OVERTURNED AND TORN APART. A FEW TREES WERE BLOWN DOWN AND THE FOUNDATION OF A CONCRETE BLOCK HOUSE WAS DAMAGED.

THE EF2 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN IN SWEDEN...AND CONTINUED 16.9 MILES TO 3.45 NE OF LODGE CORNER...AFFECTING JEFFERSON AND ARKANSAS COUNTIES. IN JEFFERSON COUNTY...NUMEROUS TREES WERE BLOWN DOWN. GRAIN BINS WERE DESTROYED...AND THERE WAS DAMAGE TO METAL BUILDINGS AND OVERTURNED FARM MACHINERY. IN ARKANSAS COUNTY...NUMEROUS TREES WERE BLOWN DOWN...AND TWO MOBILE HOMES WERE DAMAGED.
LAW ENFORCEMENT REPORTED A TORNADO ON THE GROUND AT THE JUNCTION OF HIGHWAYS 11 AND 165 TWO MILES EAST OF LODGE CORNER.
TORNADO REPORTED ON THE GROUND BY STORM CHASERS 4 MILES NORTHWEST OF DE WITT.

THE EF2 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN 5.5 MILES WNW OF DE WITT...AND CONTINUED 14.4 MILES TO NEAR CROCKETTS BLUFF. THIS TORNADO REMAINED IN ARKANSAS COUNTY...WITH FOUR STEEL TRANSMISSION TOWERS BLOWN DOWN...AND THERE WAS DAMAGE TO A FEW FARM OUTBUILDINGS. NUMEROUS TREES WERE BLOWN DOWN...AND A TRAVEL TRAILER WAS BLOWN OVER. AN ELEVATOR WAS BLOWN OFF OF GRAIN BINS.
TORNADO REPORTED ON THE GROUND BY STORM CHASERS 4 MILES NORTHWEST OF DE WITT.
A SHED AND DUCK BLIND WERE DEMOLISHED ON STROH ROAD.
TORNADO REPORTED ON THE GROUND 1 MILE WEST OF BURKS.
TRAILER HOUSE DESTROYED ON GAROT ROAD SOUTH OF DE WITT.

AN EF1 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN 8.2 MILES SOUTH OF DE WITT...AND CONTINUED 9.4 MILES TO 3 MILES SE OF DE WITT. THIS TORNADO REMAINED IN ARKANSAS COUNTY...WITH TREES...POWER LINES AND POWER POLES BLOWN DOWN. SEVERAL CARPORTS AND SHEDS WERE BLOWN AWAY. A LARGE LIMB FELL ON THE CAB OF A PICKUP TRUCK. A HOUSE HAD THE WINDOWS BLOWN OUT AND PIECES OF WOOD WEDGED INTO THE SIDGING. A MOBILE HOME WAS DESTROYED. A METAL BUILDING WAS DESTROYED...AND A ROOF WAS BLOWN OFF OF A TRACTOR SHED.

TORNADO STARTED 4 MILES SOUTH OF RAGTOWN AND ENDED 3.2 MILES EAST OF RAGTOWN AT THE COUNTY BORDER. THE TOTAL PATH LENGTH OF THE STORM WAS 5.8 MILES AND WAS RATED AN EF1. DAMAGE FOUND INCLUDED BLOWN DOWN TREES AND POWER LINES. AN IRRIGATION PIVOT WAS FLIPPED OVER AND A LARGE STORAGE SHED WAS TOSSED OVER A FARM BUILDING.
HAIL BETWEEN THE SIZE OF DIMES AND QUARTERS ARE COVERING THE GROUND ON HIGHWAY 9 BETWEEN CROWS AND PARON.
HAIL RANGING FROM DIME SIZED TO QUARTER SIZED ARE CURRENTLY FALLING.
TREES AND POWERLINES ARE DOWN IN BISMARK.
TREES AND POWERLINES ARE DOWN IN MALVERN.
SEVERAL TREES WERE BLOWN DOWN ALONG 46 SOUTH.
SEVERAL TREES ARE DOWN IN THE HUMNOKE AREA.

ESTIMATED 60-70 MPH WINDS CAUSED DAMAGE IN A 2-MILE SWATH ACROSS WALDRON. DOZENS OF TREES AND POWER LINES WERE BLOWN DOWN...AS WELL AS STREET LIGHTS. SEVERAL HOUSES HAD DAMAGE...PARTLY DUE TO TREES FALLING ON THEM. SOME HOMES HAD SHINGLES BLOWN OFF OF ROOFS...AS WELL AS AWNINGS DAMAGED. THE ROOFS OF TWO BARNS WERE BLOWN OFF. THE DAMAGE WAS ALL IN A CONTINUOUS PATH...WITH DAMAGE ALL IN A SOUTHWEST TO NORTHEAST DIRECTION. THE TIME IS BASED ON RADAR...WHICH SHOWED A STRAIGHT-LINE WIND SIGNATURE.
HAIL RANGED IN SIZE FROM PENNY TO QUARTER.
SOME TREE DEBRIS WAS ON LOCAL ROADWAYS. A WALL CLOUD WAS REPORTED AS THE STORM WENT BY.
MULTIPLE TREES AND POWER LINES ARE DOWN IN CAMDEN AND ACROSS PORTIONS OF OUACHITA COUNTY. TIME IS ESTIMATED OFF OF RADAR.

A FENCE WAS BLOWN DOWN AND THERE WAS DAMAGE TO THE FASCIA OF A CHURCH.

UPDATE FROM PREVIOUS LSR. TREES WERE BLOWN DOWN AND SEVERAL BLOCKED ROADS. A BUSINESS SIGN WAS BLOWN DOWN ONTO A VEHICLE. THE VEHICLE WAS NOT OCCUPIED. SEVERAL CITY STREETS HAD MINOR FLOODING. SEVERAL HOMES AND ONE CHURCH OFF HWY 165 COMING INTO ENGLAND HAD WATER UP TO THE DOORS. ENGLAND SCHOOL DISTRICT DISMISSED STUDENTS EARLY DUE TO THE HAZARDOUS ROAD CONDITIONS. SEVERAL OTHER STREETS, HWY 31,HWY 15, AND HWY 13 HAD REPORTS OF FLOODING.

A POSSIBLE BRIEF TORNADO WAS SPAWNED. A MOBILE HOME AND SEVERAL SHEDS WERE DAMAGED OR DESTROYED. ONE INJURY WAS REPORTED. NUMEROUS TREES WERE DOWNED...WITH SOME TREES ON HOUSES AND VEHICLES. TREES WERE ALSO BLOCKING ROADS.
A POSSIBLE BRIEF TORNADO WAS SPAWNED. SEVERAL HOUSES AND MOBILE HOMES WERE DAMAGED. SOME INJURIES WERE BEING REPORTED. NUMEROUS TREES WERE DOWNED AND WERE BLOCKING ROADS.

*** 1 INJ *** AN EF1 TORNADO HAS BEEN CONFIRMED BY SURVEY TEAM. A DOUBLE WIDE MOBILE HOME WAS DESTROYED. SURVEY TEAM IS CONTINUING THE SURVEY AND MORE DETAILS WILL BECOME AVAILABLE LATER THIS AFTERNOON.
